sentences.sentence: Shipbuilding today is based on science and ships are built using computers and sophisticated tools.
sentences.translation: 今天的造船以科学为基础，船舶的建造会使用计算机和精密工具。

structure.text: and ships are built using computers and sophisticated tools

difficult_points: and 连接两个并列分句，分别说明现代造船的理论基础和具体工具。
are built 是被动语态，强调船舶被建造的方式而不是建造者。
using computers and sophisticated tools 是现在分词短语，补充说明建造手段。

sentences.sentence: Shipbuilding in ancient Rome, however, was more of an art relying on estimation, inherited techniques and personal experience.
sentences.translation: 然而，古罗马的造船更像是一门技艺，依靠估算、继承下来的技术和个人经验。

structure.text: relying on estimation, inherited techniques and personal experience

difficult_points: however 插入在主语和谓语之间，表示与上一句现代造船形成对比。
more of an art 表示“更像是一门技艺”，不是说完全不是科学。
relying on 引出的分词短语说明古罗马造船依赖的依据。

sentences.sentence: The Romans were not traditionally sailors but mostly land-based people, who learned to build ships from the people that they conquered, namely the Greeks and the Egyptians.
sentences.translation: 罗马人传统上并不是水手，而主要是陆地民族；他们是从被自己征服的民族那里学会造船的，也就是从希腊人和埃及人那里学来的。

structure.text: were not traditionally sailors but mostly land-based people
who learned to build ships from the people that they conquered
namely the Greeks and the Egyptians

difficult_points: not ... but ... 结构突出罗马人的传统身份不是海员，而是陆地民族。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明罗马人如何学会造船。
that they conquered 修饰 the people，说明这些传授造船技术的人后来被罗马征服。
namely 后面具体点明 the people 指希腊人和埃及人。

expressions.meaning_zh: 以陆地生活为主的民族

sentences.sentence: There are a few surviving written documents that give descriptions and representations of ancient Roman ships, including the sails and rigging.
sentences.translation: 现存少量书面文献对古罗马船只进行了描述和图示，其中包括船帆和索具。

structure.text: There are a few surviving written documents
that give descriptions and representations of ancient Roman ships

difficult_points: There are 是存在句，核心信息是现存的 written documents。
that 引导定语从句，说明这些文献提供了什么内容。
including the sails and rigging 补充说明描述和图示覆盖的船只部件。

sentences.sentence: Excavated vessels also provide some clues about ancient shipbuilding techniques.
sentences.translation: 出土的船只也为了解古代造船技术提供了一些线索。

structure.text: some clues about ancient shipbuilding techniques

difficult_points: Excavated vessels 是过去分词作定语，表示“被发掘出来的船只”。
also 表明除书面文献外，实物船只也是证据来源。
about ancient shipbuilding techniques 修饰 clues，说明线索涉及的内容。

sentences.sentence: Studies of these have taught us that ancient Roman shipbuilders built the outer hull first, then proceeded with the frame and the rest of the ship.
sentences.translation: 对这些船只的研究使我们了解到，古罗马造船者先建造外船壳，然后再进行船架和船只其余部分的建造。

structure.text: that ancient Roman shipbuilders built the outer hull first, then proceeded with the frame and the rest of the ship
ancient Roman shipbuilders built the outer hull first
then proceeded with the frame and the rest of the ship

difficult_points: these 指代上一句的 excavated vessels，即出土船只。
that 引导宾语从句，说明研究让我们知道的具体内容。
built ... first, then proceeded ... 表示造船步骤的先后顺序。

sentences.sentence: Planks used to build the outer hull were initially sewn together.
sentences.translation: 用于建造外船壳的木板最初是缝合在一起的。

structure.text: Planks used to build the outer hull
used to build the outer hull

difficult_points: used to build the outer hull 是过去分词短语，修饰 Planks。
were sewn together 是被动语态，说明木板被缝合。
initially 表示这种连接方式只是在早期使用。

sentences.sentence: Starting from the 6th century BCE, they were fixed using a method called mortise and tenon, whereby one plank locked into another without the need for stitching.
sentences.translation: 从公元前6世纪开始，这些木板用一种叫作榫卯的方法固定起来；通过这种方法，一块木板可以嵌入另一块木板，而不需要缝合。

structure.text: using a method called mortise and tenon
whereby one plank locked into another without the need for stitching

difficult_points: they 指代上一句中的 Planks，即外船壳木板。
were fixed 是被动语态，强调木板被固定的方式。
called mortise and tenon 是过去分词短语，说明 method 的名称。
whereby 引导关系从句，表示“通过这种方式”。

sentences.sentence: Then in the first centuries of the current era, Mediterranean shipbuilders shifted to another shipbuilding method, still in use today, which consisted of building the frame first and then proceeding with the hull and the other components of the ship.
sentences.translation: 后来，在公元最初几个世纪，地中海地区的造船者转向了另一种至今仍在使用的造船方法，这种方法包括先建造船架，然后再建造船壳和船只其他部件。

structure.text: Then in the first centuries of the current era
which consisted of building the frame first and then proceeding with the hull and the other components of the ship

difficult_points: Then 和时间短语共同标明造船方法演变的下一阶段。
still in use today 是省略了 which is 的后置修饰，说明该方法沿用至今。
which 指代 another shipbuilding method，后面解释这种方法的具体流程。
building ... and then proceeding ... 是 consist of 后并列的动名词结构。

sentences.sentence: This method was more systematic and dramatically shortened ship construction times.
sentences.translation: 这种方法更加系统，并大幅缩短了船舶建造时间。

structure.text: and dramatically shortened ship construction times

difficult_points: This method 指代上一句提到的先建船架再建船壳的方法。
was 和 shortened 构成并列谓语，分别说明方法的特点和效果。
dramatically 修饰 shortened，强调缩短幅度很大。

sentences.sentence: The ancient Romans built large merchant ships and warships whose size and technology were unequalled until the 16th century CE.
sentences.translation: 古罗马人建造了大型商船和战船，其规模和技术直到公元16世纪才被匹敌。

structure.text: built large merchant ships and warships
whose size and technology were unequalled until the 16th century CE

difficult_points: whose 引导定语从句，修饰 large merchant ships and warships。
were unequalled 是被动含义的形容词结构，表示没有其他船只可以与之相比。
until the 16th century CE 表示这种领先状态持续到公元16世纪。

sentences.sentence: Warships were built to be lightweight and very speedy.
sentences.translation: 战船被建造成轻巧且速度很快的样子。

structure.text: to be lightweight and very speedy

difficult_points: were built 是被动语态，强调战船的设计结果。
to be lightweight and very speedy 表示建造目的或预期特性。
lightweight 和 speedy 是并列形容词，概括战船的核心要求。

sentences.sentence: They had to be able to sail near the coast, which is why they had no ballast or excess load and were built with a long, narrow hull.
sentences.translation: 它们必须能够在靠近海岸的地方航行，这就是为什么它们没有压舱物或多余载重，并且采用又长又窄的船壳建造。

structure.text: They had to be able to sail near the coast
which is why they had no ballast or excess load and were built with a long, narrow hull
they had no ballast or excess load and were built with a long, narrow hull

difficult_points: They 指代上一句的 Warships。
had to be able to 表示必须具备某种能力，后接不定式说明能力内容。
which is why 引出结果解释，说明靠岸航行需求带来的设计选择。
had no ... and were built ... 是并列谓语，分别说明载重和船体形状。

sentences.sentence: They did not sink when damaged and often would lie crippled on the sea's surface following naval battles.
sentences.translation: 它们受损时不会沉没，并且在海战之后常常会受损瘫痪地漂浮在海面上。

structure.text: and often would lie crippled on the sea's surface following naval battles

difficult_points: when damaged 是省略结构，完整含义是 when they were damaged。
would lie crippled 描述受损后的状态，crippled 作主语补足语。
following naval battles 表示海战之后的时间背景。

sentences.sentence: They had a bronze battering ram, which was used to pierce the timber hulls or break the oars of enemy vessels.
sentences.translation: 它们装有青铜撞角，用来刺穿敌船的木质船壳，或折断敌船的船桨。

structure.text: They had a bronze battering ram
which was used to pierce the timber hulls or break the oars of enemy vessels
to pierce the timber hulls or break the oars of enemy vessels

difficult_points: which 指代 a bronze battering ram，说明撞角的用途。
was used to 是被动结构，表示“被用来”。
pierce 和 break 是并列动词，共同说明撞角的两种攻击功能。

sentences.sentence: Warships used both wind (sails) and human power (oarsmen) and were therefore very fast.
sentences.translation: 战船同时使用风力（船帆）和人力（桨手），因此速度非常快。

structure.text: used both wind (sails) and human power (oarsmen)

difficult_points: both ... and ... 连接两种动力来源：风力和人力。
括号中的 sails 和 oarsmen 分别解释 wind 与 human power 的具体形式。
therefore 表示结果，说明双重动力带来速度优势。

sentences.sentence: Eventually, Rome's navy became the largest and most powerful in the Mediterranean, and the Romans had control over what they therefore called Mare Nostrum, meaning 'our sea'.
sentences.translation: 最终，罗马海军成为地中海地区规模最大、实力最强的海军，罗马人也控制了他们因此称为 Mare Nostrum 的海域，意思是“我们的海”。

structure.text: Rome's navy became the largest and most powerful in the Mediterranean
and the Romans had control over what they therefore called Mare Nostrum
what they therefore called Mare Nostrum

difficult_points: the largest and most powerful 是并列最高级，说明罗马海军在地中海的地位。
what 引导宾语从句，作 had control over 的宾语。
therefore 说明命名 Mare Nostrum 与罗马控制地中海之间的因果关系。
meaning 'our sea' 是分词短语，解释 Mare Nostrum 的含义。

sentences.sentence: There were many kinds of warship.
sentences.translation: 战船有许多种类。

structure.text: There were many kinds of warship

difficult_points: There were 是存在句结构，用来引出战船种类的话题。
many kinds of warship 是真正承载信息的名词短语。
warship 在 of 后作为类别概念使用。

sentences.sentence: The 'trireme' was the dominant warship from the 7th to 4th century BCE.
sentences.translation: “三列桨战船”是公元前7世纪到公元前4世纪的主导型战船。
difficult_points: dominant 表示在该时期占主导地位，而不只是数量较多。
from the 7th to 4th century BCE 标明这一地位持续的大致年代范围。
The 'trireme' 的引号表示这是专门的船型名称。

sentences.sentence: It had rowers in the top, middle and lower levels, and approximately 50 rowers in each bank.
sentences.translation: 它在上、中、下三层都有桨手，每一排大约有50名桨手。

structure.text: had rowers in the top, middle and lower levels
and approximately 50 rowers in each bank
in the top, middle and lower levels

difficult_points: It 指代上一句的 trireme。
top, middle and lower levels 说明桨手按垂直层级分布。
and 后省略了 had，表示每个 bank 也有约50名桨手。

sentences.sentence: The rowers at the bottom had the most uncomfortable position as they were under the other rowers and were exposed to the water entering through the oar-holes.
sentences.translation: 底层的桨手处境最不舒服，因为他们位于其他桨手下方，并且会接触到从桨孔进入的水。

structure.text: as they were under the other rowers and were exposed to the water entering through the oar-holes

difficult_points: at the bottom 修饰 The rowers，限定为底层桨手。
as 引导原因状语从句，解释为什么底层位置最不舒服。
were exposed to 表示“暴露于、接触到”，这里指接触进水。
entering through the oar-holes 是现在分词短语，修饰 the water。

sentences.sentence: It is worth noting that contrary to popular perception, rowers were not slaves but mostly Roman citizens enrolled in the military.
sentences.translation: 值得注意的是，与大众看法相反，桨手并不是奴隶，而大多是在军队服役的罗马公民。

structure.text: that contrary to popular perception, rowers were not slaves but mostly Roman citizens enrolled in the military
rowers were not slaves but mostly Roman citizens enrolled in the military

difficult_points: It is worth noting that 是固定表达，用来提示后面的信息值得关注。
contrary to popular perception 是插入性介词短语，表示与普遍看法相反。
not ... but ... 强调桨手不是奴隶，而多为罗马公民。
enrolled in the military 是过去分词短语，修饰 Roman citizens。

sentences.sentence: The trireme was superseded by larger ships with even more rowers.
sentences.translation: 三列桨战船后来被拥有更多桨手的大型船只取代。

structure.text: by larger ships with even more rowers

difficult_points: was superseded 是被动语态，表示这种船型被后来的船只取代。
by larger ships 表明取代者是更大的船只。
with even more rowers 修饰 larger ships，说明新船的桨手更多。

sentences.sentence: Merchant ships were built to transport lots of cargo over long distances and at a reasonable cost.
sentences.translation: 商船被建造出来，是为了以合理成本长距离运输大量货物。

structure.text: to transport lots of cargo over long distances and at a reasonable cost
over long distances and at a reasonable cost

difficult_points: were built 是被动语态，强调商船的设计目的。
to transport lots of cargo 是目的不定式，说明商船被建造的用途。
over long distances and at a reasonable cost 是并列状语，分别说明距离和成本。

sentences.sentence: They had a wider hull, double planking and a solid interior for added stability.
sentences.translation: 它们有更宽的船壳、双层木板和坚固的内部结构，以增加稳定性。

structure.text: had a wider hull, double planking and a solid interior

difficult_points: They 指代上一句的 Merchant ships。
a wider hull, double planking and a solid interior 是三项并列宾语。
for added stability 表示这些设计特征的目的，即增加稳定性。

sentences.sentence: Unlike warships, their V-shaped hull was deep underwater, meaning that they could not sail too close to the coast.
sentences.translation: 与战船不同，商船的V形船壳深在水下，这意味着它们无法驶得太靠近海岸。

structure.text: meaning that they could not sail too close to the coast
that they could not sail too close to the coast

difficult_points: Unlike warships 用介词短语引出商船与战船的对比。
their 指代 Merchant ships，说明商船的船壳特点。
meaning that ... 是现在分词短语，说明深水船壳带来的结果。
could not sail too close to the coast 表示能力受限，而不是一次具体行为。

sentences.sentence: They usually had two huge side rudders located off the stern and controlled by a small tiller bar connected to a system of cables.
sentences.translation: 它们通常有两个巨大的侧舵，位于船尾外侧，并由一根连接到缆绳系统的小舵柄控制。

structure.text: usually had two huge side rudders
controlled by a small tiller bar connected to a system of cables
connected to a system of cables

difficult_points: located off the stern 和 controlled by ... 都修饰 two huge side rudders。
controlled by 是过去分词短语，说明侧舵如何被操控。
connected to a system of cables 修饰 a small tiller bar，说明舵柄与缆绳系统相连。
off the stern 表示在船尾附近但不一定在船体内部。

sentences.sentence: They had from one to three masts with large square sails and a small triangular sail at the bow.
sentences.translation: 它们有一到三根桅杆，配有大型方帆，并在船首有一张小三角帆。

structure.text: had from one to three masts
with large square sails and a small triangular sail at the bow

difficult_points: from one to three 表示数量范围，修饰 masts。
with 引出的介词短语说明桅杆和帆的配置。
large square sails 与 a small triangular sail 形成并列，对比帆的形状和大小。
at the bow 指小三角帆所在的位置。

sentences.sentence: Just like warships, merchant ships used oarsmen, but coordinating the hundreds of rowers in both types of ship was not an easy task.
sentences.translation: 和战船一样，商船也使用桨手，但协调这两类船上数百名桨手并不是一件容易的事。

structure.text: but coordinating the hundreds of rowers in both types of ship was not an easy task
coordinating the hundreds of rowers in both types of ship

difficult_points: Just like warships 表明商船在使用桨手方面与战船相似。
but 连接转折分句，转入“协调桨手很困难”这一问题。
coordinating ... 是动名词短语作主语，谓语是 was not an easy task。
both types of ship 指商船和战船两类船。

sentences.sentence: In order to assist them, music would be played on an instrument, and oars would then keep time with this.
sentences.translation: 为了帮助他们，人们会用乐器演奏音乐，船桨随后会跟着这种节奏保持同步。

structure.text: music would be played on an instrument
and oars would then keep time with this

difficult_points: In order to assist them 是目的状语，them 指需要协调的桨手。
would be played 是被动语态，表示音乐会被演奏出来。
keep time with this 表示船桨按照音乐节奏同步动作。
this 指代前面提到的 music 或音乐节奏。

sentences.sentence: The cargo on merchant ships included raw materials (e.g. iron bars, copper, marble and granite), and agricultural products (e.g. grain from Egypt's Nile valley).
sentences.translation: 商船上的货物包括原材料（例如铁条、铜、大理石和花岗岩）以及农产品（例如来自埃及尼罗河谷的谷物）。

structure.text: included raw materials (e.g. iron bars, copper, marble and granite), and agricultural products (e.g. grain from Egypt's Nile valley)

difficult_points: 主语是 The cargo on merchant ships，说明讨论的是商船所载货物。
included 后面并列列出 raw materials 和 agricultural products 两大类。
两个 e.g. 分别为原材料和农产品提供例子，不能误判为句子结束。
from Egypt's Nile valley 修饰 grain，说明谷物来源。

sentences.sentence: During the Empire, Rome was a huge city by ancient standards of about one million inhabitants.
sentences.translation: 在帝国时期，按照古代标准来看，罗马是一座拥有约一百万居民的巨大城市。

structure.text: was a huge city by ancient standards

difficult_points: During the Empire 提供历史时期背景。
by ancient standards 表示评价“巨大城市”的参照标准是古代社会。
of about one million inhabitants 补充说明城市人口规模。
about one million 表示约数，不是精确统计。

sentences.sentence: Goods from all over the world would come to the city through the port of Pozzuoli situated west of the bay of Naples in Italy and through the gigantic port of Ostia situated at the mouth of the Tiber River.
sentences.translation: 来自世界各地的货物会通过位于意大利那不勒斯湾以西的波佐利港，以及位于台伯河河口的巨大奥斯蒂亚港进入罗马城。

structure.text: Goods from all over the world
through the port of Pozzuoli situated west of the bay of Naples in Italy
and through the gigantic port of Ostia situated at the mouth of the Tiber River
situated at the mouth of the Tiber River

difficult_points: from all over the world 修饰 Goods，强调货物来源广泛。
would come 表示在当时经常发生的情况。
through ... and through ... 是并列介词短语，列出两条入城港口路径。
situated west ... 和 situated at ... 分别后置修饰两个港口。

sentences.sentence: Large merchant ships would approach the destination port and, just like today, be intercepted by a number of towboats that would drag them to the quay.
sentences.translation: 大型商船会接近目的港，并且就像今天一样，会被若干拖船拦截，由这些拖船把它们拖到码头。

structure.text: and, just like today, be intercepted by a number of towboats
that would drag them to the quay

difficult_points: would approach 和 be intercepted 共享主语 Large merchant ships。
just like today 是插入语，提示这种港口操作与今天相似。
be intercepted by 是被动结构，说明商船由拖船接管。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 towboats，并说明拖船的作用。

sentences.sentence: The time of travel along the many sailing routes could vary widely.
sentences.translation: 沿众多航线旅行所需的时间可能差异很大。

structure.text: The time of travel along the many sailing routes
of travel along the many sailing routes

difficult_points: The time of travel 是主语核心，指航行所需时间。
along the many sailing routes 修饰 travel，说明旅行发生在多条航线上。
could vary widely 表示可能存在很大差异，而非固定时间。

sentences.sentence: Navigation in ancient Rome did not rely on sophisticated instruments such as compasses but on experience, local knowledge and observation of natural phenomena.
sentences.translation: 古罗马的航海导航并不依赖罗盘等精密仪器，而是依赖经验、当地知识以及对自然现象的观察。

structure.text: did not rely on sophisticated instruments such as compasses but on experience, local knowledge and observation of natural phenomena
experience, local knowledge and observation of natural phenomena

difficult_points: not ... but ... 表示不是依赖精密仪器，而是依赖经验和观察。
such as compasses 举例说明 sophisticated instruments。
experience, local knowledge and observation 是三项并列宾语。
of natural phenomena 修饰 observation，说明观察对象。

sentences.sentence: In conditions of good visibility, seamen in the Mediterranean often had the mainland or islands in sight, which greatly facilitated navigation.
sentences.translation: 在能见度良好的情况下，地中海的海员常常能看到大陆或岛屿，这极大地方便了导航。

structure.text: often had the mainland or islands in sight

difficult_points: In conditions of good visibility 是条件背景，说明在能见度好的时候。
had ... in sight 表示“看得见……”。
which 指代前面“能看到大陆或岛屿”这一整件事。
greatly facilitated navigation 说明可见陆地对导航的帮助很大。

sentences.sentence: They sailed by noting their position relative to a succession of recognisable landmarks.
sentences.translation: 他们通过记录自己相对于一连串可识别地标的位置来航行。

structure.text: by noting their position relative to a succession of recognisable landmarks
relative to a succession of recognisable landmarks

difficult_points: They 指代上一句中的 seamen。
by noting ... 表示航行所采用的方法。
relative to 表示位置关系，即相对于地标而言。
a succession of recognisable landmarks 指连续出现、可以识别的地标。

sentences.sentence: When weather conditions were not good or where land was no longer visible, Roman mariners estimated directions from the pole star or, with less accuracy, from the Sun at noon.
sentences.translation: 当天气状况不好，或者看不到陆地时，罗马水手会根据北极星估计方向，或者在准确度较低的情况下，根据正午的太阳估计方向。

structure.text: When weather conditions were not good or where land was no longer visible
where land was no longer visible
from the pole star or, with less accuracy, from the Sun at noon

difficult_points: When ... or where ... 并列两个条件：天气不好和看不见陆地。
no longer visible 表示陆地不再可见，强调由可见变为不可见。
from the pole star 和 from the Sun at noon 是并列方向判断依据。
with less accuracy 是插入性介词短语，说明依据太阳判断方向不如北极星准确。

sentences.sentence: They also estimated directions relative to the wind and swell.
sentences.translation: 他们还会根据风向和浪涌来估计方向。

structure.text: directions relative to the wind and swell
relative to the wind and swell

difficult_points: They 指代 Roman mariners，即罗马水手。
also 表示这是除北极星和太阳之外的另一种估计方式。
relative to the wind and swell 修饰 directions，说明方向判断参照风和浪涌。

sentences.sentence: Overall, shipping in ancient Roman times resembled shipping today with large vessels regularly crossing the seas and bringing supplies from their Empire.
sentences.translation: 总体而言，古罗马时期的航运与今天的航运相似，大型船只会定期横渡海洋，并从帝国各地运来补给。

structure.text: with large vessels regularly crossing the seas and bringing supplies from their Empire
regularly crossing the seas and bringing supplies from their Empire

difficult_points: Overall 表示总结整段关于古罗马航运的描述。
shipping in ancient Roman times 是主语，resembled 表示与现代航运相似。
with large vessels ... 是伴随结构，说明相似性的具体表现。
crossing 和 bringing 是并列现在分词，描述大型船只的常规活动。
